title: Recording versus alerting rules
description: Recording rules precompute series on a schedule; alerting rules evaluate a condition and emit alerts.
body: Use when deciding which rule type to author. A recording rule evaluates an expression on an interval and stores the result as a new series for cheap reuse; an alerting rule evaluates a condition and, once true for its 'for' duration, sends an alert to Alertmanager. Do not embed heavy expressions directly in alert rules — back them with recording rules. Actions: precompute with recording rules and reference them from alerts. Validation signals include alerts firing only after the 'for' window. Risk boundary: a missing 'for' duration makes alerts flap on transient spikes.
